hero.subtitle: AI isn't a separate tab: it lives inside recruiting, people and payroll. It prepares and proposes the operational work — drafting, extracting, calculating, summarizing — and the final wording and the final decision are always yours.

demo.liveBadge: Interactive · pick a task and a tone
demo.disclaimerPost: . The final wording and the final decision are yours — it never publishes or hires on its own.

texts.directo: We're hiring a Senior Product Designer in Madrid. You'll design end-to-end flows for HR teams. You need 5+ years, strong Figma and systems thinking. Range €55–75k. Hybrid, 2 office days.
texts.calido: Passionate about design with purpose? Join us as a Senior Product Designer in Madrid and shape the experience of thousands of HR teams. We're looking for 5+ years and a love of systems. €55–75k, hybrid, and a team that cares.
texts.formal: TalentOS seeks to appoint a Senior Product Designer in Madrid. Responsibility: design of end-to-end HR workflows. Requirements: 5+ years of experience and proficiency in Figma. Compensation: €55,000–75,000. Hybrid arrangement.

texts.directo: Elena Vidal scores 95/100. 6 years in B2B product, led an HRIS redesign. Strength: design systems. To validate: stakeholder management. In the interview, ask about her research process.
texts.calido: Great profile! Elena Vidal fits at 95/100. She brings 6 years in B2B product and an HRIS redesign to be proud of. She shines at design systems; worth exploring how she handles stakeholders. A chat about her research will clear it up.
texts.formal: Candidate: Elena Vidal. Fit score: 95/100. Experience: 6 years in B2B product, including leading an HRIS redesign. Key strength: design systems. To verify: stakeholder management.

texts.directo: With €1,500 for Backend Sr, put 60% into LinkedIn: average fit 78 vs 61 and the best cost per hire. Keep 25% on InfoJobs for volume and hold 15% in reserve. Review in 7 days.
texts.calido: With your €1,500 for Backend Sr, I'd bet big on LinkedIn (60%): it brings the best fit, 78 versus 61. 25% on InfoJobs adds volume, and I'd keep 15% just in case. Let's look at it together in a week.
texts.formal: Budget: €1,500 for the Backend Sr opening. Recommended allocation: LinkedIn 60% (average fit 78 vs 61), InfoJobs 25% (volume), reserve 15%. Suggested review in 7 days.

rows.text: It drafts, extracts, calculates and summarizes — the mechanical work that eats hours.
With scope, evidence and one concrete action — never a "maybe you should".
You confirm or discard with equal weight. The final wording is human. It never acts alone.

invariante.procBody: Every result says where it came from. No black boxes: you know whether it was a model, a heuristic or an estimate.

procRows.text: A language model was behind it.
A deterministic fallback rule — never hidden.
An approximate value, labeled as such.

cards.name: Job post writer
cards.desc: Writes the description and defines the profile from four inputs, with a tone selector.

cards.name: AI career site
cards.desc: Generates your entire public page from an intake — hero, culture, benefits and FAQ.

cards.name: Company profile
cards.desc: Extracts your company profile from your website so you never start from scratch.

cards.name: Candidate analysis
cards.desc: Summarizes the CV, computes an explainable fit and suggests what to ask in the interview.

cards.name: Channel optimizer
cards.desc: Recommends where to invest based on budget, volume or quality — and discusses it with you.

cards.name: Dashboard insights
cards.desc: Flags what's urgent with scope, evidence and an action. You mark it done or ignore it.

cards.name: Onboarding checklist
cards.desc: Builds the onboarding plan tailored to the person's role and team.

cta.title1: Leave the mechanical work to the agents.
cta.body: They already live inside every flow — there's nothing to "switch on". Start for free and see for yourself.
